description: GPT4All is represented as a local desktop application that can run on end-user consumer devices.

sources.title: nomic-ai/gpt4all GitHub API response
sources.quote: "description":"GPT4All: Run Local LLMs on Any Device. Open-source and available for commercial use."

description: Jan is represented as a local desktop application that can run on end-user consumer devices.

sources.title: Jan GitHub repository
sources.quote: - **Privacy First**: Everything runs locally when you want it to

description: Stable Diffusion web UI is represented as a local image-generation application suitable for high-end consumer NVIDIA GPUs.

sources.title: Stable Diffusion web UI GitHub repository
sources.quote:
Make sure the required [dependencies](https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui/wiki/Dependencies) are met and follow the instructions available for:
- [NVidia](https://github.com/AUTOMATIC1111/stable-diffusion-webui/wiki/Install-and-Run-on-NVidia-GPUs) (recommended)

description: llama.cpp supports local inference on CUDA-capable consumer GPUs represented by the RTX 5090 profile.

sources.title: llama.cpp GitHub repository
sources.quote: - Custom CUDA kernels for running LLMs on NVIDIA GPUs (support for AMD GPUs via HIP and Moore Threads GPUs via MUSA)

description: llama.cpp supports Apple Silicon local inference paths represented by the Apple M5 Max profile.

sources.title: llama.cpp GitHub repository
sources.quote: - Apple silicon is a first-class citizen - optimized via ARM NEON, Accelerate and Metal frameworks

description: Ollama is represented as supporting local model serving on CUDA-capable consumer GPUs.

sources.title: Ollama hardware support documentation
sources.quote: Ollama supports Nvidia GPUs with compute capability 5.0+ and driver version 531 and newer. Nvidia GPUs with compute capability 5.0 through 6.2 require driver version 570 or newer.

description: Ollama is represented as supporting local model serving on Apple Silicon systems.

sources.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
sources.quote: This results in a large speedup of Ollama on all Apple Silicon devices. On Apple’s M5, M5 Pro and M5 Max chips, Ollama leverages the new GPU Neural Accelerators to accelerate both time to first token (TTFT) and generation speed (tokens per second).

description: PyTorch supports NVIDIA GPU training and inference paths represented by the H100 profile.

sources.title: PyTorch CUDA documentation
sources.quote: This package adds support for CUDA tensor types. It implements the same function as CPU tensors, but they utilize GPUs for computation.

description: PyTorch supports AMD GPU training and inference paths through ROCm, represented by the MI300X profile.

sources.title: PyTorch ROCm documentation
sources.quote: ROCm\ |trade| is AMD’s open source software platform for GPU-accelerated high performance computing and machine learning. HIP is ROCm's C++ dialect designed to ease conversion of CUDA applications to portable C++ code. HIP is used when converting existing CUDA applications like PyTorch to portable C++ and for new projects that require portability between AMD and NVIDIA.

description: PyTorch can target Cloud TPU environments through PyTorch/XLA, represented by the TPU v6e Trillium profile.

sources.title: PyTorch/XLA documentation
sources.quote:
``torch_xla`` is a Python package that implements \
`XLA <https://openxla.org/xla>`_ as a backend for PyTorch.

+------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------+------------------------------------------------+
| **Familiar APIs**                              | **High Performance**                           | **Cost Efficient**                             |
|                                                |                                                |                                                |
| Create and train PyTorch models on TPUs,       | Scale training jobs across thousands of        | TPU hardware and the XLA compiler are optimized|
| with only minimal changes required.            | TPU cores while maintaining high MFU.          | for cost-efficient training and inference.     |

description: PyTorch can target AWS Trainium through the Neuron software stack, represented by the Trainium3 profile.

sources.title: PyTorch Support on Neuron
sources.quote: PyTorch support on AWS Neuron SDK - TorchNeuron Native for eager execution and torch.compile on Trainium and Inferentia, with torch-neuronx XLA-based support for training and inference.

description: PyTorch is represented as a training and inference framework that can target Intel Gaudi accelerator environments through Intel's Gaudi software stack.

sources.title: Getting Started with Training on Intel Gaudi
sources.quote: This guide provides simple steps for preparing a PyTorch model to run training on Intel® Gaudi® AI accelerator.

description: vLLM is represented as supporting high-throughput serving on NVIDIA H100 datacenter GPU hardware.

sources.title: gpt-oss vLLM Usage Guide
sources.quote: In vLLM, you can run it on NVIDIA H100, H200, B200 as well as MI300x, MI325x, MI355x and Radeon AI PRO R9700.

description: vLLM is represented as supporting high-memory NVIDIA H200 datacenter GPU serving.

sources.title: gpt-oss vLLM Usage Guide
sources.quote: In vLLM, you can run it on NVIDIA H100, H200, B200 as well as MI300x, MI325x, MI355x and Radeon AI PRO R9700.

description: vLLM is represented as supporting AMD ROCm serving paths on high-memory MI325X-class hardware.

sources.title: gpt-oss vLLM Usage Guide
sources.quote: In vLLM, you can run it on NVIDIA H100, H200, B200 as well as MI300x, MI325x, MI355x and Radeon AI PRO R9700.

description: Triton Inference Server is represented as supporting NVIDIA datacenter inference GPUs, with the B200 node serving as the Blackwell profile.

sources.title: Triton Inference Server GitHub repository
sources.quote:
Triton Inference Server supports inference across cloud, data center, edge and embedded
devices on NVIDIA GPUs, x86 and ARM CPU, or AWS Inferentia. Triton Inference
Server delivers optimized performance for many query types, including real time,
batched, ensembles and audio/video streaming.

description: DeepSpeed is represented as supporting distributed GPU training and fine-tuning on NVIDIA Hopper-class datacenter accelerators.

sources.title: DeepSpeed GitHub repository
sources.quote:
Specific GPUs we develop and test against are listed below, this doesn't mean your GPU will not work if it doesn't fall into this category it's just DeepSpeed is most well tested on the following:
  * NVIDIA: Pascal, Volta, Ampere, and Hopper architectures

description: Megatron-LM is represented as optimized for large-scale NVIDIA GPU training systems.

sources.title: Megatron-LM GitHub repository
sources.quote: - **[2025/08]** **[Mixture of Experts (MoE) Q3–Q4 2025 Roadmap](https://github.com/NVIDIA/Megatron-LM/issues/1729)** - Comprehensive roadmap for MoE features including DeepSeek-V3, Qwen3, advanced parallelism strategies, FP8 optimizations, and Blackwell performance enhancements.

description: Unsloth local fine-tuning workflows are represented as supporting NVIDIA RTX 50-series consumer GPU profiles, including the RTX 5090 profile.

sources.title: Unsloth GitHub repository
sources.quote: * **NVIDIA:** Training works on RTX 30/40/50, Blackwell, DGX Spark, Station and more

description: The SDXL model card documents CUDA inference and GPU VRAM constraints; the RTX 5090 node represents the high-end local CUDA GPU profile used by the seed stack.

sources.title: Stable Diffusion XL Base 1.0 model card
sources.quote:
If you are limited by GPU VRAM, you can enable *cpu offloading* by calling `pipe.enable_model_cpu_offload`
instead of `.to("cuda")`:

description: Local Llama 3.1 8B inference requires an end-user device with enough memory for the selected quantization and runtime.

sources.title: Llama 3.1 8B Instruct model card
sources.note: The linked source does not provide edge-specific evidence for model:llama-3.1-8b-instruct requires infrastructure:consumer-device; this relationship is source-needed until a direct quote is collected.
